course_title: Chinese to English Translation: An Introductory Course
course_description:
This course is designed for native speakers of Mandarin Chinese and its dialects who speak English as a second language. It introduces to students the fundamental skills in Chinese to English translation through a variety of media and genres, both oral and written, drawn from sources including television, film, and formal and informal written texts. Specific training will be given in the selection of context appropriate English expression. The course is divided into modules designed to give students training in the translation of different genres including texts drawn from areas such as the popular press, literature, fiction and film. Students will be introduced to general skills required for Chinese to English translation and translation revision, utilising theoretical concepts and contrastive linguistic analysis, translation issues relating to each genre and specific translation issues raised by texts under study. Students will be guided through the production of a linguistically and culturally appropriate, idiomatic translation of the source text. This course is not recorded.

The course is divided into modules designed to give students training in the translation of different genres including texts drawn from areas such as the popular press,ﾠ literature,ﾠ fictionﾠ andﾠ film. Students will be introduced to generalﾠskills required for Chinese to English translationﾠ and translation revision, utilising theoretical concepts and contrastive linguistic analysis,ﾠ translation issues relating to each genre ﾠand specific translation issues raised by texts under study. Students will be guided through the production of a linguistically and culturally appropriate, idiomatic translation of the source text. This course is not recorded.ﾠ

incompatible: You can't enrol in this course if you've already completed the following:
restrictions: Restricted to students who are native speakers of Chinese.

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
You attend one 2-hour "workshop" class per week (there is no separate lecture or tutorial).
Public holidays:
Alternative arrangements for affected students will be announced through the Blackboard site.
Class allocation:
In order to optimise the student experience, it may be necessary to reallocate students to a different class from their first choice.
Before this happens, every effort will be made to enable students to voluntarily change into an alternative class that is suitable. Please note: Teaching staff do not have access to the timetabling system to help with class allocation.
Therefore, should you need help with your timetable and/or allocation of classes, please ensure you email
hass.mytimetable@uq.edu.au
from your UQ student email account with the following details:
Full name,
Student ID, and
the Course Code
Additional information and support can be found
here
course_aims: ﾠThis course aims to introduce to native speakers of Chinese the fundamental issues concerning translation and translation revision of common genres from Chinese into English.

learning_outcomes.description: translate/revise common genres from Chinese into idiomatic English in a linguistically and culturally appropriate manner
access and understand the application of a variety of translation resources and conduct translation revision
have a basic grasp of Hanyu Pinyin
understand traditional and simplified character Chinese texts
have a basic linguistic vocabulary to talk about texts

assessment_details.title: Weekly e-quizzes on Bb
assessment_details.due_date: WEEK 2 - 6/03/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 4 - 20/03/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 6 - 2/04/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 7 - 17/04/2026 5:00 pm WEEk 8 - 24/04/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 9 - 1/05/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 10 - 8/05/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 11 - 15/05/2026 5:00 pm WEEK 12 - 22/05/2026 5:00 pm Please note: Week 6 - Due at 5pm on Thursday, as the Friday is a public holiday.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Type: E-quizzes on Blackboard Reading: 0 minutes Duration: 20 minutes Format: Translation and multiple-choice Starting from Week 2, each Monday, an online quiz will be posted on the "Assessment" page on Blackboard, which you must complete before Friday 5 pm of the same week. On three "odd" [单数] week (Weeks 7, 9 & 11) , the online quiz is a practical translation revision task (consisting of 4-8 sentences) based on the contents of the preceding week(s) and/or that week. On an "even" [双数] week (Weeks 2, 4, 6, 8, 10 &12 ), the online quiz is a multiple choice quiz (consisting of 8 questions) that tests what is discussed in that week's and the preceding week's Online Modules. There is only 1 correct answer for each of the 8 questions. The best 8 e-quiz results for each student will be used to calculate the final mark for this assessment item. You must complete an e-quiz in 20 minutes without interruption . So, be sure to use reliable internet, e.g. UQL. No reading time is allowed. AI and MT Statement This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit via Blackboard link.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
No extensions are allowed for this series of tasks as students are given a seven-day period to complete them, and only the best 8 out of 9 results will be used to calculate final mark for this assessment task. Individual results and correct answers are automatically released on a weekly basis. Timely engagement with these tasks supports sequential nature of learning in this course.
Late submission
Late submissions not accepted, as the task remains open for one week.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessment_details.late_submission: Late submissions not accepted, as the task remains open for one week.

assessment_details.title: ONLINE In-semester (School) Blackboard Exam (60 minutes)
assessment_details.due_date: 2/04/2026 Please note that the exam starts at 7:30 pm.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The in-semester assessment will test students' knowledge and understanding of all the W1-W5 course content, particularly the in-class materials. Students will translate seen texts or texts similar to the in-class materials (25% of exam content) and an unseen text (75% of exam content), based on Newspaper Texts 1 & 2, and Film Texts 1 & 2. The unseen text is approximately 320 characters in length. The texts will represent the different genres studied in W1-W5 (i.e. sign, news, film). Students may have access to lecture notes and paper dictionaries during the exam. The exam is online on Blackboard. Be sure to access a reliable internet connection. It is strongly recommended you save your work on a Word file as well. You will have 60 minutes of working time plus 10 minutes for planning. Your time begins once you start the exam, and it must be completed in a single sitting. You may use any printed materials or online tools to complete this assessment, but getting help from other people is strictly prohibited. AI and MT Statement This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.exam_details: Planning time 10 minutes Duration 60 minutes Calculator options No calculators permitted Open/closed book Open book examination - any written or printed material is permitted; material may be annotated Exam platform Other Invigilation Not invigilated
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The exam is automatically submitted.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to defer this exam .
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam .

assessment_details.title: Oral interview (sight translation)
assessment_details.due_date: 8/06/2026 - 9/06/2026 Each student will be assigned a time slot in Exam WEEK 1 Monday or Tuesday. If students have time clashes with other exams, please reach out to your class teacher for a rearrangement.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The final oral is an in-person sight translation task. It will test students' knowledge and understanding and application of the course content, particularly the in-class materials. Students will be required to sight translate one Chinese text into spoken English. Each document is around 300 characters long and will be handed out at the assessment venue. E-dictionaries that work offline will be provided. Students will have 10 minutes to prepare for sight translating each document and need to finish the task within the time limit of 7 minutes. AI and MT Statement This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
Each student must complete the task within 7 minutes. The teacher or examiner may terminate the exam at the 8-minute mark.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .
assessment_details.late_submission: Each student must complete the task within 7 minutes. The teacher or examiner may terminate the exam at the 8-minute mark.

learning_activities.topic: Week 1: ONLINE MODULE 1 + course introduction Overview of course requirements and content. Introduction to key issues in Chinese>English translation. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L06
Week 2: ONLINE MODULE 2 + Newspaper Text 1 Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
Week 3: ONLINE MODULE 3 + Film Text 1 Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
Week 4: ONLINE MODULE 4 + Newspaper Text 2 Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
Week 5: ONLINE MODULE 5 + Film Text 2 Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
Week 6: ONLINE Mid-semester Exam + Exam Review NO ONLINE MODULE IN WEEK 6. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
Week 7: ONLINE MODULE6 + Newspaper Text 3 Revision Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
Week 8: ONLINE MODULE7 + Mid-semester Exam Reflection + Sight Translation Text 1 + Comic Book Text 1 started (if time permits). NO CLASS ON 18 APRIL (GOOD FRIDAY)-- MAKE-UP CLASS TBA.
Week 9: ONLINE MODULE 8 + Sight Translation Texts 2 & 3 Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
Week 10: ONLINE MODULE9 + Newspaper Text4 Revision + Sight Translation Text 4 NO CLASS ON 5 MAY (LABOUR DAY) -- MAKE-UP CLASS TBA. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
Week 11: ONLINE MODULE 10 + Literary Texts 1 + Sight Translation Text 5 Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
Week 12: ONLINE MODULE 11 + Film Text 3 Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
Week 13: Review for Final Oral Review (Sight translation) NO ONLINE MODULE IN WEEK 13 Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
